Notes: UAT for the Set 052 cost-dashboard redesign (read-path fix + router-capability tier gate + staleness banner + three honest states). UAT was elected at session start (suggestion_disposition: uat, session 1). Items the S3 orchestrator could verify programmatically (unit tests, manifest inspection, source inspection) are marked Passes=true with the verification path in Feedback. Items requiring live VS Code interaction in a real Full-routing workspace vs a Lightweight workspace — the rendered icon, the painted banner, clicking 'Update cost estimates' and watching router-config.yaml open at the right line — are flagged 'Manual operator UAT' and left Passes=false / Result=pending for the operator to walk through post-publish. NOTE on the deferred Layer-3 deviation: S2 pivoted the planned Playwright icon-visibility smoke to a deterministic manifest gate-wiring guard (costDashboardGate.test.ts) because VS Code view/title actions duplicate-render in the DOM and overflow past the first action, making them non-deterministic to assert in Playwright (no codebase precedent; mirrors migration-cta-v4.spec.ts). The live icon-visibility items below (UAT-1, UAT-2) are precisely the coverage that deviation deferred to manual UAT.

Review.ItemLabel: Cost icon + command present when the workspace routes
Review.HumanAction: Open a Full-tier workspace that carries `ai_router/router-config.yaml`. Look at the Session Sets view title bar for the cost-dashboard icon, and open the Command Palette and type 'Show Cost Dashboard'.
Review.Expectation: The cost-dashboard `view/title` icon is visible (possibly under the `...` overflow in a narrow sidebar) and `Dabbler: Show Cost Dashboard` is offered in the Command Palette. Invoking it opens the dashboard panel.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — live icon rendering. The gate WIRING is unit-verified by `costDashboardGate.test.ts` (both the view/title and commandPalette manifest entries reference `dabblerSessionSets.routesCost`) and the predicate by `routerConfig.test.ts::routesCost`, but the painted icon was deliberately not asserted in Playwright (S2 D7 deviation — view/title actions are non-deterministic in the DOM).

Review.ItemLabel: Cost icon + command ABSENT on Lightweight
Review.HumanAction: Open a Lightweight workspace (no `ai_router/router-config.yaml`). Check the Session Sets view title bar and the Command Palette for the cost dashboard.
Review.Expectation: No cost-dashboard icon in the title bar; `Dabbler: Show Cost Dashboard` does NOT appear in the Command Palette. The cost surface simply does not exist on Lightweight.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — confirms the negative case the gate produces. `routerConfig.test.ts` proves `routesCost()` returns false when only an empty `ai_router/` (or none) is present (folder existence alone is insufficient); the menu `when`-clause that consumes the context key is pinned by `costDashboardGate.test.ts`.

Review.ItemLabel: Dashboard reads the file the router actually writes
Review.HumanAction: In a workspace where the router has logged calls, confirm `ai_router/router-metrics.jsonl` exists and contains JSONL rows, then open the Cost Dashboard.
Review.Expectation: The dashboard renders real cumulative spend / per-set / sparkline / model-mix from `router-metrics.jsonl` — NOT an empty panel. (Pre-Set-052 it read a hardcoded `ai_router/metrics.jsonl` the router never wrote, so it always looked dead.)
Review.Feedback: Read-path resolution is unit-verified by `routerConfig.test.ts` (resolves `metrics.log_filename`, defaults to `router-metrics.jsonl`, honors a custom filename) and `metrics.test.ts` (reader routes through the resolver). Live render of populated data is recommended as an operator spot-check but the resolution logic is deterministically covered.

Review.HumanAction: Set `metrics.log_filename: my-metrics.jsonl` in `router-config.yaml`; ensure that file holds rows. Open the dashboard.
Review.Expectation: The dashboard reads `ai_router/my-metrics.jsonl` (the resolver follows the config, with no second hardcoded name anywhere). The empty-state panel's 'Reading from <path>' line shows the resolved path.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `routerConfig.test.ts` (custom `log_filename` resolution) and `dashboardHtml.test.ts` (empty-state path interpolation). The reader and the CSV export both route through the single `routerConfig.ts` resolver.

Review.ItemLabel: CSV export populates the session column
Review.HumanAction: With logged data, use the dashboard's CSV export and open the file.
Review.Expectation: The session column is populated (the schema field is `session_number`). Pre-Set-052 the export keyed off `session_num`, which the router never emits, so that column was silently blank. `adjudication` bookkeeping rows (no model, zero cost) are excluded.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `metrics.test.ts` (MetricsEntry reconciled to the on-disk schema: `session_number`, optional `call_type`, reader drops `adjudication` rows). Operator spot-check of an actual export recommended.

Review.ItemLabel: Stale rates surface a non-blocking banner
Review.HumanAction: Set `metadata.pricing_reviewed` to a date older than `metadata.review_frequency_days` (default 30) days ago. Open the Cost Dashboard.
Review.Expectation: A non-blocking '⚠ Cost estimates may be stale' banner appears above the data/empty content with an 'Update cost estimates' button, naming the age in days and the threshold. The dashboard content is still shown (non-blocking).
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — live banner render. The staleness predicate (stale when age > frequency) and the banner copy are unit-verified by `routerConfig.test.ts::computeStaleness` and `dashboardHtml.test.ts::stalenessBannerHtml` (present/age-text/threshold). Painted appearance in a real panel is the manual portion.

Review.HumanAction: Set `metadata.pricing_reviewed` to today (or within the review frequency). Open the dashboard.
Review.Expectation: No staleness banner is shown — the dashboard renders cost content cleanly.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `routerConfig.test.ts` (fresh → stale=false) and `dashboardHtml.test.ts` (banner builder returns empty string when not stale, so the renderer injects nothing).

Review.HumanAction: Remove `metadata.pricing_reviewed` (or set it to a non-date string). Open the dashboard.
Review.Expectation: The banner appears and says cost estimates have no recorded review date (unknown age). A freshly-bootstrapped config prompts a review rather than silently claiming the rates are current.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `routerConfig.test.ts` (missing/invalid → stale=true, ageDays=null) and `dashboardHtml.test.ts` (unknown-age banner copy).

Review.ItemLabel: metrics.enabled == false names the REAL knob
Review.HumanAction: Set `metrics.enabled: false` in `router-config.yaml`. Open the dashboard.
Review.Expectation: The panel says metrics logging is off and to set `metrics.enabled: true` in `router-config.yaml`. It NEVER mentions a `config.py METRICS_ENABLED` flag (which does not exist). An 'Open router-config.yaml' button is present.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `dashboardHtml.test.ts` (disabled-state copy asserts no `METRICS_ENABLED`/`config.py` string and that `metrics.enabled` is named) and `routerConfig.test.ts::selectCostState` (metricsEnabled false → 'disabled'). Live render recommended as a spot-check.

Review.ItemLabel: Metrics on but no rows yet is distinct from disabled
Review.HumanAction: With `metrics.enabled` on (default) but an empty/absent metrics log, open the dashboard.
Review.Expectation: The panel says metrics are enabled but no routed calls have been recorded yet, and shows the resolved 'Reading from <path>' line — distinct copy from the disabled state. The staleness banner (if stale) is shown above it.
Review.Feedback: Covered by `routerConfig.test.ts::selectCostState` (entryCount<=0 with metrics on → 'empty') and `dashboardHtml.test.ts` (empty-state copy + path interpolation + banner injection).

Review.ItemLabel: Recorded rows render the full dashboard
Review.HumanAction: With recorded metrics rows, open the dashboard.
Review.Expectation: Cumulative spend, per-set breakdown, 30-day sparkline, model mix, and CSV export all render against the real data.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — full data render is the existing dashboard surface (unchanged charting). `selectCostState` (entryCount>0 → 'data') is unit-covered; the operator confirms the charts paint with real router-metrics.jsonl data.

Review.ItemLabel: No state ever names the fictional config.py METRICS_ENABLED flag
Review.HumanAction: Inspect every non-data panel state (no-workspace, no-router, disabled, empty).
Review.Expectation: None of the panels reference `config.py` or `METRICS_ENABLED`. The disabled state names the real `metrics.enabled` knob in `router-config.yaml`.
Review.Feedback: Unit-verified by `dashboardHtml.test.ts` which asserts `!/METRICS_ENABLED/` across the non-data states. The S2 fix also removed the fictional-flag copy from `webview/dashboard.html`.

Review.ItemLabel: 'Update cost estimates' opens router-config.yaml at the metadata block
Review.HumanAction: With a stale banner showing, click 'Update cost estimates'.
Review.Expectation: `router-config.yaml` opens in the editor revealed at the `pricing_reviewed:` line (or the `metadata:` header if that key is absent), so the operator can update the review date / rates in place.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — live editor reveal. Anchor resolution (`findConfigAnchorLine` prefers `pricing_reviewed:`, falls back to `metadata:`) is unit-verified by `dashboardHtml.test.ts`. The actual document-open + cursor-reveal is the manual portion.

Review.ItemLabel: 'Open router-config.yaml' from the disabled state targets metrics.enabled
Review.HumanAction: From the disabled state (`metrics.enabled: false`), click 'Open router-config.yaml'.
Review.Expectation: `router-config.yaml` opens revealed at the `metrics.enabled` line so the operator can flip it on.
Review.Feedback: Manual operator UAT — live editor reveal. `findConfigAnchorLine(text, 'metrics')` line-targeting is unit-verified by `dashboardHtml.test.ts`. Document-open + reveal is the manual portion. Button wiring is CSP-safe (delegated click, no inline onclick).

Review.ItemLabel: Extension still activates and the Session Sets tree renders with the new context-key wiring
Review.HumanAction: Open any workspace; confirm the extension activates without errors and the Session Sets view renders its buckets.
Review.Expectation: Clean activation; tree renders; no manifest-registration errors from the new `dabblerSessionSets.routesCost` context key or gated menu entries.
Review.Feedback: Covered by the Layer-3 `session-sets-tree.spec.ts` (5/5 passing with the new context-key wiring) and `tsc --noEmit` clean. 584 unit tests pass (2 pre-existing Set-026 failures in untouched files).
